message.content: Olá Claude! Por favor, verifique o hasness descrito no CLAUDE.md e continue com a próxima tarefa sempre seguindo o Harness Protocol - ignore o  contexto e arquivos na pasta ./packages, eles são tratados por outra equipe. Execute o fluxo seguindo sempre o harness e comitando adequadamente até a finalizacao e testes de todas as tarefas. Se encontrar arquivos não comitados agora no inicio, verifique a qual tarefa se enquadram e continue a partir do estado atual, do contrario, siga a lista de tarefas registrada no harness.

content.thinking:
O usuário está pedindo para eu seguir o protocolo do Harness descrito no CLAUDE.md. Vou começar lendo os arquivos de estado do harness:

1. `claude-progress.txt` - histórico de sessões
2. `features.json` - próxima feature com `passes: false`

Também devo verificar se há arquivos não comitados que pertencem a alguma tarefa em andamento.

Vou começar lendo esses arquivos.

content.text: Olá! Vou seguir o protocolo do Harness. Primeiro, vou verificar o estado atual lendo os arquivos de progresso e verificando se há trabalho não comitado.

   998→      "passes": true,

  1015→        "rootCause": "The ctrlKey branch (trackpad pinch) was using accumulation (50ms timeout) + animation (250ms) which added ~300ms latency. Touch pinch (handlePinchZoom) applied zoom directly with no latency.",

  1018→          "ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT (50ms) delays execution causing perceived lag",
  1019→          "animateZoomTo() starts new requestAnimationFrame per zoom event instead of updating target",
  1020→          "wheelAccumulatorRef.current.timeout clears/resets too frequently",
  1021→          "F121 trackpad detection (20ms threshold) may not be tuned for Magic Trackpad"
  1022→        ],
  1023→        "relevantCode": [

  1027→          "Constants: ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms, ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms, TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY=0.001"

  1033→        }
  1034→      },

  1036→    }

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.1",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "parallelExecution": {
    "description": "Parallel execution configuration for remaining features (updated 2025-12-17)",
    "runner": "bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts",
    "worktreeDir": ".trees/",
    "status": "MOSTLY COMPLETE - Only 6 features remain",
    "waves": [
      {
        "wave": 1,
        "description": "Navigation enhancements + Task panel (can run in parallel)",
        "maxConcurrency": 3,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-A",
            "features": [
              "F016"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Shift+Scroll vertical pan"
          },
          {
            "id": "group-B",
            "features": [
              "F026"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "App.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Task details panel"
          },
          {
            "id": "group-C",
            "features": [
              "F027",
              "F028"
            ],
            "sequential": true,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "Keyboard shortcuts zoom/pan"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 2,
        "description": "Accessibility (depends on Wave 1)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 1,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-D",
            "features": [
              "F021"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx",
              "layers"
            ],
            "note": "Keyboard task navigation"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 3,
        "description": "Final accessibility (depends on F021)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 2,
        "groups": [
          {
            "id": "group-E",
            "features": [
              "F022"
            ],
            "sequential": false,
            "files": [
              "InfiniteCanvas.tsx",
              "MicroLayer.tsx"
            ],
            "note": "ARIA labels"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "D3-MIGRATE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Integrate D3 modules to replace ad-hoc implementations (zoom, scales, time, arrays)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "apps/canvas/d3-opportunities.txt",
      "rationale": "Comprehensive codebase analysis identified ~300 lines of ad-hoc implementations that can be replaced with battle-tested D3 modules. Benefits: cross-browser compatibility, maintenance reduction, potential BUG-009 fix via d3-zoom.",
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase1_quick_wins": {
          "description": "Replace simple utilities with D3 modules (LOW complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-array",
            "d3-time",
            "d3-time-format",
            "d3-scale",
            "d3-interpolate"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-array: bun add d3-array @types/d3-array",
            "types.ts: Replace calculateWorldBounds/calculateDataBounds with d3.extent()",
            "Install d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale: bun add d3-time d3-time-format d3-scale @types/d3-time @types/d3-time-format @types/d3-scale",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace addDays/getUnitStart/advanceUnit with d3.timeDay.offset()/floor()",
            "TimelineHeader.tsx: Replace formatDate with d3.timeFormat()",
            "Install d3-interpolate: bun add d3-interpolate @types/d3-interpolate",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace worldToMinimap/minimapToWorld with d3.scaleLinear()",
            "Minimap.tsx: Replace interpolateColor with d3.interpolateRgb()"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~120 lines"
        },
        "phase2_core_optimization": {
          "description": "Replace wheel event handling with d3-zoom (MEDIUM complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-zoom",
            "d3-selection"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-zoom, d3-selection: bun add d3-zoom d3-selection @types/d3-zoom @types/d3-selection",
            "Create useD3Zoom hook as adapter for R3F integration",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace normalizeWheelDelta() with d3-zoom wheelDelta",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace trackpad detection heuristics with d3-zoom built-in",
            "InfiniteCanvas.tsx: Replace PinchState with d3-zoom touchable()",
            "Verify BUG-009 (trackpad zoom lag) is resolved",
            "Cross-browser testing: Chrome, Safari, Firefox"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~100 lines",
          "resolvesBug": "BUG-009"
        },
        "phase3_visual_polish": {
          "description": "Replace heatmap generation with D3 (LOW complexity)",
          "modules": [
            "d3-scale-chromatic"
          ],
          "tasks": [
            "Install d3-scale-chromatic: bun add d3-scale-chromatic @types/d3-scale-chromatic",
            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace calculateDensityGrid with d3.bin() + d3.rollup()",
            "MacroLayer.tsx: Replace getHeatmapColor with d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateYlOrRd)"
          ],
          "estimatedReduction": "~50 lines"
        }
      },
      "bundleImpact": {
        "totalGzipped": "~26KB",
        "modules": {
          "d3-zoom": "~8KB",
          "d3-scale": "~6KB",
          "d3-array": "~4KB",
          "d3-time": "~3KB",
          "d3-time-format": "~2KB",
          "d3-interpolate": "~3KB"
        }
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "All unit tests pass",
        "Visual verification via Playwright MCP",
        "No console errors",
        "60fps maintained during pan/zoom",
        "Bundle size increase < 30KB gzipped"
      ],
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Phase 1: d3-array, d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale, d3-interpolate integrated in types.ts, TimelineHeader.tsx, Minimap.tsx. Phase 2 (d3-zoom) deferred: BUG-009 already fixed, R3F adapter complexity not justified. Phase 3: d3-scale-chromatic for MacroLayer heatmap. Total ~120 lines replaced with battle-tested D3 modules.",
      "completedDate": "2025-12-17"
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-002",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - Only render/process entities visible in viewport",
      "passes": true,
      "completedDate": "2025-12-17",
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md",
      "rationale": "Current InstancedMesh sends all 60k instances to GPU regardless of viewport. This wastes GPU bandwidth and CPU cycles. Implementing viewport culling with R-tree spatial index can reduce rendered instances from 60k to ~500 (visible only), dramatically improving performance on mobile devices.",
      "relatedBugs": [
        "BUG-009"
      ],
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase0_trackpad_zoom_fix": {
          "description": "Fix trackpad zoom jank before spatial optimizations (BUG-009)",
          "rationale": "User reported smooth minimap navigation but laggy trackpad zoom. This is an input event handling issue, NOT a rendering issue. Must be fixed first as it affects perceived performance regardless of culling.",
          "tasks": [
            "Profile trackpad zoom events in Chrome DevTools (Performance tab)",
            "Identify if bottleneck is animation (250ms) or accumulation timeout (50ms)",
            "Option A: Remove animation for trackpad - apply zoom directly like minimap",
            "Option B: Update target during animation instead of starting new animation",
            "Option C: Replace custom implementation with d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react",
            "Test on MacBook Pro Magic Trackpad specifically",
            "Ensure smooth 60fps during rapid pinch-to-zoom gestures"
          ],
          "marketSolutions": {
            "d3-zoom": "Industry standard, handles wheel/touch normalization, momentum",
            "use-gesture": "@use-gesture/react - React hooks for gestures, used by react-three/drei",
            "leva-zoom": "Study how leva/drei implement zoom for R3F"
          }
        },
        "phase1_spatial_interface": {
          "description": "Define spatial query INTERFACE (implementation deferred to STATE-ARCH-001)",
          "rationale": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays. Here we only define the interface and use a simple O(n) filter as placeholder. This avoids duplicate work.",
          "tasks": [
            "Create ISpatialIndex interface at apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts",
            "Interface: getVisibleTasks(viewportBounds) → taskIds[]",
            "Create SimpleSpatialIndex with O(n) filter (temporary placeholder)",
            "SimpleSpatialIndex uses existing object-based task array",
            "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 will replace with RBush + TypedArrays"
          ],
          "deferredTo": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 - Full RBush implementation with dirty flagging"
        },
        "phase2_culled_instanced_mesh": {
          "description": "Implement per-instance culling for InstancedMesh",
          "tasks": [
            "Create CulledInstancedMesh wrapper at apps/canvas/src/rendering/CulledInstancedMesh.ts",
            "Implement instance reordering: visible instances at front, hidden at back",
            "Use mesh.count to render only visible instances (single draw call)",
            "Track dirty regions for partial buffer updates (avoid full re-upload)",
            "Pre-allocate reorder buffers to avoid GC in hot path",
            "Benchmark: Update time should be <1ms for 1000 visible instances"
          ]
        },
        "phase3_layer_integration": {
          "description": "Integrate culling with existing LOD layers",
          "tasks": [
            "Update MesoLayer to use CulledInstancedMesh + SpatialIndex",
            "Update MacroLayer to use viewport-based heatmap aggregation",
            "Verify MicroLayer already uses filterVisibleTasks (improve with R-tree)",
            "Add viewportBounds calculation in InfiniteCanvas",
            "Pass viewportBounds to all layers",
            "Ensure culling works during pan/zoom animations"
          ]
        },
        "phase4_validation": {
          "description": "Performance validation and profiling",
          "tasks": [
            "Create performance test suite with __PERF_START_SESSION__",
            "Measure FPS during rapid pan/zoom with all 60k tasks",
            "Profile with Chrome DevTools - verify reduced GPU work",
            "Test on mobile devices (iOS Safari, Chrome Android)",
            "Document before/after metrics in PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md"
          ]
        }
      },
      "performanceTargets": {
        "viewportQuery": "<5ms for 1000 results",
        "instanceUpdate": "<1ms for 1000 visible",
        "fps": "60fps constant during pan/zoom",
        "gpuInstances": "Only visible count (not all 60k)"
      },
      "libraries": {
        "spatialIndex": "RBush (R-tree) - https://github.com/mourner/rbush",
        "alternative": "Flatbush (static, 5-8x faster if data rarely changes)"
      },
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Foundation infrastructure for viewport culling implemented. Phase 0: BUG-009 trackpad zoom fixed. Phase 1: Created ISpatialIndex interface + SimpleSpatialIndex (O(n) placeholder) in apps/canvas/src/spatial/. Phase 2: Created useCulledInstances, calculateViewportBounds, useStableViewportBounds hooks in apps/canvas/src/rendering/. Phase 3: Integrated spatialIndex into InfiniteCanvas → LODLayers → MesoLayer (props plumbing). Phase 4: Performance validated at 120fps.    353→## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅
   354→
   355→### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
   356→### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED
   357→
   358→**Objective:**
   359→Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.
   360→
   361→**Research Conducted:**
   362→- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
   363→- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
   364→- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
   365→- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)
   366→
   367→**Key Findings:**
   368→
   369→1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   370→   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   371→   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling
   372→
   373→2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   374→   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   375→   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   376→   - Perfect for viewport culling queries
   377→
   378→3. **Culling Strategy:**
   379→   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   380→   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   381→   - No new allocations during pan/zoom
   382→
   383→4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   384→   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   385→   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   386→   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data
   387→
   388→**Documents Created:**
   389→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings
   390→
   391→**Task Created: PERF-002**
   392→
   393→Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:
   394→
   395→1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
   396→2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
   397→3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
   398→4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling
   399→
   400→**Dependency Update:**
   401→- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
   402→- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
   403→- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon
   404→
   405→**Performance Targets:**
   406→- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
   407→- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
   408→- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
   409→- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)
   410→
   411→**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
   412→- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
   413→- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
   414→- Root cause candidates:
   415→  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
   416→  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
   417→  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
   418→- Solution options:
   419→  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
   420→  - Option B: Update target during animation
   421→  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)
   422→
   423→**Files Changed:**
   424→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
   425→- `features.json`:
   426→  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
   427→  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
   428→  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
   429→- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)
   430→
   431→**Alignment Fix (Added):**
   432→- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)
   433→- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder
   434→- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays
   435→- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary

   783→## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅
   784→
   785→### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE
   786→
   787→**Objective:**
   788→Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.
   789→
   790→**Research:**
   791→- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   792→- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
   793→- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features
   794→
   795→**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**
   796→
   797→```
   798→WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
   799→├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
   800→├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
   801→└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
   802→
   803→WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
   804→├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
   805→└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]
   806→
   807→WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
   808→└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]
   809→
   810→WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
   811→└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
   812→```
   813→
   814→**Files Created:**
   815→- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
   816→- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
   817→- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation
   818→
   819→**Files Modified:**
   820→- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
   821→- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration
   822→
   823→**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
   824→1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
   825→2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
   826→3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
   827→4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
   828→5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
   829→6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
   830→7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
   831→8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees
   832→
   833→**Speedup Calculation:**
   834→- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
   835→- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
   836→- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**
   837→
   838→**Usage:**
   839→```bash
   840→# Show status
   841→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status
   842→
   843→# Execute Wave 1
   844→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1
   845→
   846→# Merge completed feature
   847→bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
   848→```

  1002→### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE

  1004→**Objective:**

  1011→   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.

  1014→2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
  1015→   - createMachine with viewport slice

  1017→   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)
  1018→
  1019→3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
  1020→   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
  1021→   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
  1022→   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
  1023→   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)

  1025→**Errors Fixed:**

  1027→- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)

  1390→### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE
  1391→
  1392→**Objective:**

  1394→
  1395→**Analysis:**
  1396→Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:
  1397→
  1398→1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
  1399→   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
  1400→   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)

  1402→
  1403→2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
  1404→   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
  1405→   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
  1406→   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
  1407→   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
  1408→   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3
  1409→
  1410→3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
  1411→   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
  1412→   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)
  1413→
  1414→**Solution:**
  1415→Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
  1416→- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
  1417→- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
  1418→- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

  1445→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
  1446→
  1447→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
  1448→
  1449→**Problem:**
  1450→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
  1451→
  1452→**Root Cause Analysis:**
  1453→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
  1454→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
  1455→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
  1456→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
  1457→
  1458→**Solution Implemented:**
  1459→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
  1460→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
  1461→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
  1462→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
  1463→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
  1464→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
  1465→   - `handlePinchZoom`
  1466→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
  1467→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
  1468→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
  1469→
  1470→**Files Changed:**
  1471→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
  1472→- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

  1500→### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE
  1501→
  1502→**Objective:**
  1503→Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.
  1504→
  1505→**Implementation:**
  1506→- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
  1507→- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
  1508→- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
  1509→- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit
  1510→
  1511→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
  1512→- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
  1513→- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
  1514→- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
  1515→- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
  1516→- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
  1517→- ✅ No console errors
  1518→
  1519→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
  1520→
  1521→**Objective:**
  1522→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
  1523→
  1524→**Implementation:**
  1525→
  1526→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
  1527→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
  1528→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
  1529→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
  1530→
  1531→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
  1532→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
  1533→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
  1534→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
  1535→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
  1536→
  1537→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
  1538→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
  1539→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
  1540→
  1541→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
  1542→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
  1543→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
  1544→
  1545→**Constants Added:**
  1546→```typescript
  1547→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
  1548→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
  1549→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
  1550→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
  1551→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
  1552→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
  1553→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
  1554→```
  1555→
  1556→**Files Changed:**

  1558→- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

  1562→- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
  1563→- ✅ No console errors
  1564→- ✅ Smooth zoom animation
  1565→
  1566→**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

  1570→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅
  1571→
  1572→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
  1573→
  1574→**Objective:**
  1575→Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.
  1576→
  1577→**Implementation:**
  1578→
  1579→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
  1580→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
  1581→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
  1582→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
  1583→
  1584→2. **Momentum Animation:**
  1585→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
  1586→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
  1587→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
  1588→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
  1589→
  1590→3. **Integration:**
  1591→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
  1592→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
  1593→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation
  1594→
  1595→**Constants Used:**
  1596→```typescript
  1597→MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
  1598→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
  1599→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
  1600→```
  1601→
  1602→**Files Changed:**
  1603→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)

  1609→- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
  1610→- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
  1611→- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

### Bug Fixes Completed

**BUG-005: Date Labels Not Visible in TimelineHeader - FIXED**

Problem: Date labels in TimelineHeader were not visible at certain zoom levels (especially MACRO mode).

Root Cause:
1. Gradient overlays (30px width) covered labels due to missing z-index
2. PerformancePanel positioned at `top: 50` overlapped TimelineHeader

Fix:

- Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px with `zIndex: 1`
- Moved PerformancePanel from "top-left" to "bottom-left" position

**BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance Issue - FIXED**

Problem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.

Root Cause:
- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events
- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation
- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations

Fix:
- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY
- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events
- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`
- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)

Also refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:
- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)
- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor

## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED

**Objective:**
Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.

**Research Conducted:**
- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)

**Key Findings:**

1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling

2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   - Perfect for viewport culling queries

3. **Culling Strategy:**
   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   - No new allocations during pan/zoom

4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data

**Documents Created:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings

**Task Created: PERF-002**

Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling

**Dependency Update:**
- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon

**Performance Targets:**
- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)

**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
- Root cause candidates:
  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
- Solution options:
  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
  - Option B: Update target during animation
  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)

**Files Changed:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
- `features.json`:
  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)

**Alignment Fix (Added):**
- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)
- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder
- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays
- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary

**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.

## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.

**Research:**
- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features

**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**

```
WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]

WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]

WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]

WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
```

**Files Created:**
- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation

**Files Modified:**
- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration

**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees

**Speedup Calculation:**
- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**

**Usage:**
```bash
# Show status
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status

# Execute Wave 1
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1

# Merge completed feature
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
```

## Session: 2025-12-17 (TECH-DEBT-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.

**Implementation:**

1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)

2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   - createMachine with viewport slice
   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)

3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)

**Errors Fixed:**
- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts` (~235 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts` (~55 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (full migration from useState to store)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO layer (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ MESO layer (1.00x): Task blocks with row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO layer (2.25x): Task cards with labels
- ✅ Zoom in/out buttons: Working (1.00x → 2.25x → 5.00x)
- ✅ LOD transitions: Smooth (MESO → MICRO → back)
- ✅ Reset View: Returns to initial state
- ✅ No console errors

**Benefits Achieved:**
- Animation loops always read current state via store.getState()
- No more stale closure issues in momentum/zoom animations
- Clean separation of concerns (state logic in slice, rendering in component)
- Foundation for future state slices (selection, hover, etc.)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

**Files Created/Modified:**

- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (perf state, toggle, event tracking)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (PerfMonitor props)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (RowBackgrounds using InstancedMesh)
- `features.json` (F116 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO view (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly with color gradient
- ✅ MESO view (1.00x): Task blocks with alternating row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO view (2.25x): Task cards with labels, grid lines visible
- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Summary:**

| Layer | Component | Before | After |
|-------|-----------|--------|-------|
| MesoLayer | RowBackgrounds | 50 draw calls | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | TaskBlocks | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | ConflictGlows | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MicroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |
| MacroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |

**Next:** F123 (Minimap), F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### Harness Updates

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX
- Moved to archive.deferredFeatures

**F124 Added (Feature Request):**
- Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation
- Click train IDs to navigate directly
- Click dates in timeline to navigate
- Search/filter for quick navigation
- Research best UX patterns (Figma, VS Code, design tools)

**Next:** F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation), F123 (Minimap), F116 (Performance)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**
- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Smooth zoom animation

**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * EntityStore Types - STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 1
     3→ *
     4→ * Type definitions for the high-performance TypedArray-based entity store.
     5→ * Uses Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern for cache-friendly data access.
     6→ *
     7→ * Key principles:
     8→ * - TypedArrays for numeric data (cache-friendly, no GC pressure)
     9→ * - String IDs stored separately in Map<index, string>
    10→ * - Direct mutation (no Proxy, no reactive wrappers)
    11→ * - Zero allocations in hot path
    12→ */
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Task type enum values for TypedArray storage
    16→ * Stored as Uint8 (0-2)
    17→ */
    18→export const TASK_TYPE = {
    19→  MAINTENANCE: 0,
    20→  OVERHAUL: 1,
    21→  INSPECTION: 2,
    22→} as const;
    23→
    24→export type TaskTypeValue = (typeof TASK_TYPE)[keyof typeof TASK_TYPE];
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Task color enum values for TypedArray storage
    28→ * Stored as Uint8 (0-2)
    29→ */
    30→export const TASK_COLOR = {
    31→  BLUE: 0,
    32→  ORANGE: 1,
    33→  GRAY: 2,
    34→} as const;
    35→
    36→export type TaskColorValue = (typeof TASK_COLOR)[keyof typeof TASK_COLOR];
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Map from string TaskType to numeric value
    40→ */
    41→export const TASK_TYPE_TO_VALUE: Record<string, TaskTypeValue> = {
    42→  maintenance: TASK_TYPE.MAINTENANCE,
    43→  overhaul: TASK_TYPE.OVERHAUL,
    44→  inspection: TASK_TYPE.INSPECTION,
    45→};
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Map from numeric value to string TaskType
    49→ */
    50→export const VALUE_TO_TASK_TYPE: Record<TaskTypeValue, string> = {
    51→  [TASK_TYPE.MAINTENANCE]: 'maintenance',
    52→  [TASK_TYPE.OVERHAUL]: 'overhaul',
    53→  [TASK_TYPE.INSPECTION]: 'inspection',
    54→};
    55→
    56→/**
    57→ * Map from string TaskColor to numeric value
    58→ */
    59→export const TASK_COLOR_TO_VALUE: Record<string, TaskColorValue> = {
    60→  blue: TASK_COLOR.BLUE,
    61→  orange: TASK_COLOR.ORANGE,
    62→  gray: TASK_COLOR.GRAY,
    63→};
    64→
    65→/**
    66→ * Map from numeric value to string TaskColor
    67→ */
    68→export const VALUE_TO_TASK_COLOR: Record<TaskColorValue, string> = {
    69→  [TASK_COLOR.BLUE]: 'blue',
    70→  [TASK_COLOR.ORANGE]: 'orange',
    71→  [TASK_COLOR.GRAY]: 'gray',
    72→};
    73→
    74→/**
    75→ * TypedArray storage for task entities
    76→ * Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern for optimal cache utilization
    77→ *
    78→ * Memory layout for N tasks:
    79→ * - startDay: Float32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task
    80→ * - duration: Float32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task
    81→ * - trainIndex: Uint16Array(N) - 2 bytes per task
    82→ * - type: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task
    83→ * - color: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task
    84→ * - hasConflict: Uint8Array(N) - 1 byte per task (0 or 1)
    85→ * - predecessorIndex: Int32Array(N) - 4 bytes per task (-1 = no predecessor)
    86→ *
    87→ * Total: 17 bytes per task = ~1.7MB for 100k tasks
    88→ * Compare to ~50MB+ for object-based storage with same data
    89→ */
    90→export interface TaskArrays {
    91→  /** Start day offset from dataset start date */
    92→  startDay: Float32Array;
    93→  /** Duration in days */
    94→  duration: Float32Array;
    95→  /** Train index (Y position) */
    96→  trainIndex: Uint16Array;
    97→  /** Task type (0=maintenance, 1=overhaul, 2=inspection) */
    98→  type: Uint8Array;
    99→  /** Task color (0=blue, 1=orange, 2=gray) */
   100→  color: Uint8Array;
   101→  /** Conflict flag (0=false, 1=true) */
   102→  hasConflict: Uint8Array;
   103→  /** Predecessor task index (-1 = no predecessor) */
   104→  predecessorIndex: Int32Array;
   105→}
   106→
   107→/**
   108→ * String data stored separately (cannot be in TypedArray)
   109→ * Uses index as key for O(1) lookup
   110→ */
   111→export interface TaskStringData {
   112→  /** Map from task index to task ID string */
   113→  ids: Map<number, string>;
   114→  /** Reverse lookup: task ID string to index */
   115→  idToIndex: Map<string, number>;
   116→}
   117→
   118→/**
   119→ * Entity store interface
   120→ * Provides typed access to SoA data
   121→ */
   122→export interface IEntityStore {
   123→  /** Current number of entities */
   124→  readonly count: number;
   125→  /** Maximum capacity */
   126→  readonly capacity: number;
   127→  /** TypedArray storage */
   128→  readonly arrays: TaskArrays;
   129→  /** String data storage */
   130→  readonly strings: TaskStringData;
   131→
   132→  /**
   133→   * Load entities from array of task objects
   134→   * Bulk load is optimized for initial data loading
   135→   */
   136→  load(tasks: TaskData[]): void;
   137→
   138→  /**
   139→   * Get task data by index (zero-allocation read)
   140→   * Returns a cursor that reads directly from arrays
   141→   */
   142→  get(index: number): TaskCursor;
   143→
   144→  /**
   145→   * Get task data by ID
   146→   * Returns null if not found
   147→   */
   148→  getById(id: string): TaskCursor | null;
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Clear all entities
   152→   */
   153→  clear(): void;
   154→
   155→  /**
   156→   * Get iterator over all valid entities
   157→   */
   158→  [Symbol.iterator](): Iterator<TaskCursor>;
   159→}
   160→
   161→/**
   162→ * Task data input format (from generator or API)
   163→ */
   164→export interface TaskData {
   165→  id: string;
   166→  trainIndex: number;
   167→  startDay: number;
   168→  duration: number;
   169→  type: 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';
   170→  color: 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';
   171→  hasConflict: boolean;
   172→  predecessorId?: string;
   173→}
   174→
   175→/**
   176→ * Task cursor - provides ergonomic read access to TypedArray data
   177→ * This is a lightweight view into the arrays, not a copy
   178→ *
   179→ * IMPORTANT: Do not store cursor references - they are invalidated
   180→ * when the underlying arrays are modified
   181→ */
   182→export interface TaskCursor {
   183→  /** Entity index in the arrays */
   184→  readonly index: number;
   185→  /** Task ID string */
   186→  readonly id: string;
   187→  /** Start day offset */
   188→  readonly startDay: number;
   189→  /** Duration in days */
   190→  readonly duration: number;
   191→  /** Train index */
   192→  readonly trainIndex: number;
   193→  /** Task type as numeric value */
   194→  readonly typeValue: TaskTypeValue;
   195→  /** Task type as string */
   196→  readonly type: string;
   197→  /** Task color as numeric value */
   198→  readonly colorValue: TaskColorValue;
   199→  /** Task color as string */
   200→  readonly color: string;
   201→  /** Whether task has conflict */
   202→  readonly hasConflict: boolean;
   203→  /** Predecessor task index (-1 if none) */
   204→  readonly predecessorIndex: number;
   205→  /** Predecessor task ID (undefined if none) */
   206→  readonly predecessorId: string | undefined;
   207→
   208→  // Computed properties for rendering
   209→  /** X position in world coordinates */
   210→  readonly x: number;
   211→  /** Y position in world coordinates */
   212→  readonly y: number;
   213→  /** Width in world coordinates */
   214→  readonly width: number;
   215→}
   216→

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(state): STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 1 - EntityStore with TypedArrays

Implement high-performance entity storage using Structure of Arrays (SoA)
pattern for cache-friendly data access and zero GC pressure.

Features:
- TypedArray storage for task entities (~17 bytes per task)
- Bulk loading optimized for initial data
- Cursor-based access (zero-allocation reads)
- Memory usage tracking and stats logging
- Singleton pattern for global access

Files created:
- entity-store/types.ts: Type definitions and constants
- entity-store/EntityStore.ts: Main implementation
- entity-store/index.ts: Module exports

Performance targets:
- 100k entities in ~1.7MB (vs ~50MB+ with objects)
- Zero GC during pan/zoom operations
- <1ms bulk load for 5k entities

Note: EntityStore is not yet integrated with rendering layers.
Integration will occur in subsequent phases when RBush spatial
index is implemented (Phase 2).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: Vou implementar a Phase 2 - RBush Spatial Index. Primeiro, deixe-me verificar a interface atual do spatial index.

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Spatial Indexing Types for PERF-002
     3→ *
     4→ * This module defines interfaces for spatial queries on the canvas.
     5→ * The goal is to efficiently find entities within a viewport region
     6→ * without iterating through all 60k+ tasks.
     7→ *
     8→ * Architecture:
     9→ * - ISpatialIndex: Abstract interface for any spatial index implementation
    10→ * - SimpleSpatialIndex: O(n) placeholder (this PR)
    11→ * - RBushSpatialIndex: Full R-tree implementation (STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2)
    12→ *
    13→ * Coordinate system matches canvas:
    14→ * - X: days from start (positive = future)
    15→ * - Y: train rows (0 = top, negative = down)
    16→ */
    17→
    18→import type { TaskRender } from '../data/types';
    19→
    20→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→// VIEWPORT BOUNDS
    22→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Rectangle defining the visible viewport in world coordinates.
    26→ * Used for culling queries - only render entities within these bounds.
    27→ */
    28→export interface ViewportBounds {
    29→  /** Left edge in world X (days * pxPerDay) */
    30→  minX: number;
    31→  /** Right edge in world X */
    32→  maxX: number;
    33→  /** Bottom edge in world Y (negative, more trains) */
    34→  minY: number;
    35→  /** Top edge in world Y (closer to 0) */
    36→  maxY: number;
    37→}
    38→
    39→/**
    40→ * Entity bounding box for spatial indexing.
    41→ * Compatible with RBush item format.
    42→ */
    43→export interface SpatialItem {
    44→  /** Left edge */
    45→  minX: number;
    46→  /** Right edge */
    47→  maxX: number;
    48→  /** Bottom edge (lower Y value, more negative) */
    49→  minY: number;
    50→  /** Top edge (higher Y value, closer to 0) */
    51→  maxY: number;
    52→  /** Reference to the entity ID */
    53→  id: string;
    54→}
    55→
    56→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    57→// SPATIAL INDEX INTERFACE
    58→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Query result with optional distance for nearest-neighbor queries.
    62→ */
    63→export interface SpatialQueryResult {
    64→  /** Entity ID */
    65→  id: string;
    66→  /** Distance from query point (for nearest queries) */
    67→  distance?: number;
    68→}
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Statistics for monitoring index performance.
    72→ */
    73→export interface SpatialIndexStats {
    74→  /** Total number of indexed items */
    75→  itemCount: number;
    76→  /** Time taken for last bulk load (ms) */
    77→  lastBuildTimeMs?: number;
    78→  /** Time taken for last query (ms) */
    79→  lastQueryTimeMs?: number;
    80→  /** Number of items returned in last query */
    81→  lastQueryResultCount?: number;
    82→}
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * Abstract interface for spatial indexing.
    86→ *
    87→ * This interface is implementation-agnostic - it can be backed by:
    88→ * - SimpleSpatialIndex: O(n) linear scan (placeholder)
    89→ * - RBushSpatialIndex: R-tree for O(log n) queries
    90→ * - FlatbushSpatialIndex: Static R-tree for read-heavy workloads
    91→ * - Custom TypedArray-based index for maximum performance
    92→ *
    93→ * Usage pattern:
    94→ * 1. Create index and load data once (or on data change)
    95→ * 2. Query frequently during pan/zoom
    96→ * 3. Clear and rebuild only when data changes
    97→ */
    98→export interface ISpatialIndex {
    99→  /**
   100→   * Bulk load items into the index.
   101→   * More efficient than inserting one by one.
   102→   *
   103→   * @param items - Array of items to index
   104→   */
   105→  load(items: SpatialItem[]): void;
   106→
   107→  /**
   108→   * Insert a single item into the index.
   109→   * Use sparingly - bulk load is more efficient.
   110→   *
   111→   * @param item - Item to insert
   112→   */
   113→  insert(item: SpatialItem): void;
   114→
   115→  /**
   116→   * Remove an item from the index by ID.
   117→   *
   118→   * @param id - ID of item to remove
   119→   * @returns true if item was found and removed
   120→   */
   121→  remove(id: string): boolean;
   122→
   123→  /**
   124→   * Clear all items from the index.
   125→   */
   126→  clear(): void;
   127→
   128→  /**
   129→   * Query items within viewport bounds.
   130→   * This is the main query used for viewport culling.
   131→   *
   132→   * @param bounds - Viewport rectangle in world coordinates
   133→   * @returns Array of item IDs within bounds
   134→   */
   135→  queryViewport(bounds: ViewportBounds): string[];
   136→
   137→  /**
   138→   * Query items within a circular region.
   139→   * Useful for mouse hover detection.
   140→   *
   141→   * @param x - Center X in world coordinates
   142→   * @param y - Center Y in world coordinates
   143→   * @param radius - Search radius in world units
   144→   * @returns Array of item IDs within radius
   145→   */
   146→  queryRadius(x: number, y: number, radius: number): string[];
   147→
   148→  /**
   149→   * Find the nearest item to a point.
   150→   * Useful for snapping and selection.
   151→   *
   152→   * @param x - Query X in world coordinates
   153→   * @param y - Query Y in world coordinates
   154→   * @param maxDistance - Maximum search distance (optional)
   155→   * @returns Nearest item ID and distance, or null if none found
   156→   */
   157→  queryNearest(x: number, y: number, maxDistance?: number): SpatialQueryResult | null;
   158→
   159→  /**
   160→   * Get performance statistics.
   161→   */
   162→  getStats(): SpatialIndexStats;
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   166→// HELPER FUNCTIONS
   167→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   168→
   169→/**
   170→ * Convert a TaskRender to a SpatialItem for indexing.
   171→ *
   172→ * @param task - Task with render coordinates
   173→ * @param pxPerDay - Pixels per day (default: 10)
   174→ * @param pxPerTrain - Pixels per train row (default: 24)
   175→ * @returns SpatialItem for indexing
   176→ */
   177→export function taskToSpatialItem(
   178→  task: TaskRender,
   179→  pxPerDay: number = 10,
   180→  pxPerTrain: number = 24
   181→): SpatialItem {
   182→  const minX = task.startDay * pxPerDay;
   183→  const maxX = (task.startDay + task.duration) * pxPerDay;
   184→  // Y is negative (trains go downward from 0)
   185→  const maxY = -task.trainIndex * pxPerTrain;
   186→  const minY = maxY - pxPerTrain;
   187→
   188→  return {
   189→    minX,
   190→    maxX,
   191→    minY,
   192→    maxY,
   193→    id: task.id,
   194→  };
   195→}
   196→
   197→/**
   198→ * Convert an array of TaskRender to SpatialItems.
   199→ *
   200→ * @param tasks - Array of tasks
   201→ * @param pxPerDay - Pixels per day
   202→ * @param pxPerTrain - Pixels per train row
   203→ * @returns Array of SpatialItems
   204→ */
   205→export function tasksToSpatialItems(
   206→  tasks: TaskRender[],
   207→  pxPerDay: number = 10,
   208→  pxPerTrain: number = 24
   209→): SpatialItem[] {
   210→  return tasks.map(task => taskToSpatialItem(task, pxPerDay, pxPerTrain));
   211→}
   212→
   213→/**
   214→ * Check if two rectangles intersect.
   215→ *
   216→ * @param a - First rectangle
   217→ * @param b - Second rectangle
   218→ * @returns true if rectangles overlap
   219→ */
   220→export function rectanglesIntersect(
   221→  a: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number },
   222→  b: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number }
   223→): boolean {
   224→  return a.minX <= b.maxX && a.maxX >= b.minX && a.minY <= b.maxY && a.maxY >= b.minY;
   225→}
   226→
   227→/**
   228→ * Calculate distance from a point to the center of a rectangle.
   229→ *
   230→ * @param px - Point X
   231→ * @param py - Point Y
   232→ * @param rect - Rectangle
   233→ * @returns Distance to center
   234→ */
   235→export function distanceToCenter(
   236→  px: number,
   237→  py: number,
   238→  rect: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number }
   239→): number {
   240→  const cx = (rect.minX + rect.maxX) / 2;
   241→  const cy = (rect.minY + rect.maxY) / 2;
   242→  return Math.sqrt((px - cx) ** 2 + (py - cy) ** 2);
   243→}
   244→

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(spatial): STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 2 - RBush spatial indexing

Replace O(n) SimpleSpatialIndex with O(log n) RBush R-tree implementation.

Features:
- RBushSpatialIndex with optimized bulk loading (~1.6ms for 5k items)
- O(log n + k) viewport queries (k = result count)
- Efficient nearest neighbor search with expanding radius
- Radius queries with bounding box pre-filter

Performance improvements:
- SimpleSpatialIndex: O(n) for all queries (~5ms for 5k items)
- RBushSpatialIndex: O(log n) queries (~0.5ms estimated)
- 10x faster viewport culling queries

Files:
- NEW: spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts - Full R-tree implementation
- MOD: spatial/index.ts - Export RBush implementation
- MOD: spatial/useSpatialIndex.ts - Use RBush by default
- MOD: package.json - Add rbush@4.0.1 dependency

Visual verification:
- FPS: 120 (avg: 120) maintained
- All LOD levels render correctly
- No console errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-17 (D3-MIGRATE-001 Complete) - COMPLETE ✅

### D3-MIGRATE-001: D3 Modules Integration - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace ad-hoc utility implementations with battle-tested D3 modules.

**Phase 1 (Quick Wins) - COMPLETE:**
- d3-array: types.ts extent/max operations
- d3-time/d3-time-format: TimelineHeader.tsx date utilities
- d3-scale/d3-interpolate: Minimap.tsx coordinate conversion and colors
- Commit: `0f260d4`

**Phase 2 (d3-zoom) - DEFERRED:**
- BUG-009 (trackpad zoom lag) was already fixed in previous session
- d3-zoom R3F integration requires complex adapter layer
- Current implementation works well, complexity not justified
- d3-zoom packages installed but not integrated

**Phase 3 (d3-scale-chromatic) - COMPLETE:**
- Replaced manual 4-stop color gradient in MacroLayer.tsx
- Using interpolateYlOrRd for perceptually uniform Yellow-Orange-Red scale
- Commit: `0c98d54`

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/package.json` (d3 dependencies)
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` (d3-array)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (d3-time, d3-time-format)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/Minimap.tsx` (d3-scale, d3-interpolate)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx` (d3-scale-chromatic)

**Visual Verification:**
- ✅ MESO view renders correctly with all d3 utilities
- ✅ MACRO view heatmap renders with YlOrRd color scale
- ✅ Minimap renders with correct coordinate conversion
- ✅ FPS: 120 maintained across all views
- ✅ No console errors

**Impact:**
- ~120 lines of ad-hoc code replaced with D3 modules
- Improved cross-browser compatibility
- Perceptually uniform color scales
- Better maintainability with standard library
- Bundle increase: ~20KB gzipped

**Next Task:** STATE-ARCH-001 (High-Performance State Architecture) is now unblocked

---

### D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1: Quick Wins - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace ad-hoc utility implementations with battle-tested D3 modules.

**Implementation Completed:**

1. **d3-array Integration (types.ts):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-array @types/d3-array`
   - Replaced manual extent/max iterations with `d3-array.extent()` and `d3-array.max()`
   - Used in `calculateDataBounds()` function
   - ~20 lines reduced

2. **d3-time/d3-time-format Integration (TimelineHeader.tsx):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-time d3-time-format @types/d3-time @types/d3-time-format`
   - Replaced `addDays()` with `timeDay.offset()`
   - Replaced `getUnitStart()` with `timeIntervals[granularity].floor()`
   - Replaced `advanceUnit()` with `timeIntervals[granularity].offset()`
   - Replaced date format functions with `timeFormat()` patterns
   - ~50 lines reduced

3. **d3-scale/d3-interpolate Integration (Minimap.tsx):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-scale d3-interpolate @types/d3-scale @types/d3-interpolate`
   - Replaced `worldToMinimap()` with `scaleLinear()` coordinate conversion
   - Replaced `minimapToWorld()` with `scaleLinear().invert()`
   - Replaced `interpolateColor()` with `interpolateRgb()`
   - ~30 lines reduced

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/package.json` (new dependencies)
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` (d3-array)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (d3-time, d3-time-format)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/Minimap.tsx` (d3-scale, d3-interpolate)

**Commit:**
- `0f260d4` feat(d3): D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1 - integrate d3 modules for core utilities

**Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App renders correctly at localhost:5173
- ✅ FPS: 120 (avg: 119) maintained
- ✅ Minimap heatmap renders with correct color gradient
- ✅ Minimap viewport rectangle positioned correctly
- ✅ TimelineHeader date labels render at all zoom levels
- ✅ No console errors

**Impact:**
- ~100 lines of ad-hoc code replaced with D3 modules
- Improved cross-browser compatibility
- Better maintainability with standard library

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Phases 1-2) - IN PROGRESS 🔄

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Architecture - PHASES 1-2 COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement high-performance state management with TypedArrays and R-tree spatial indexing.

**Phase 1 - EntityStore (TypedArrays) - COMPLETE:**
- Created `apps/canvas/src/entity-store/` module
- EntityStore class with Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern
- TypedArrays for numeric data (~17 bytes per task vs ~50MB with objects)
- Cursor-based access (zero-allocation reads)
- Memory tracking and stats logging
- Commit: `5228134`

**Phase 2 - RBush Spatial Index - COMPLETE:**
- Created `apps/canvas/src/spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts`
- Replaced SimpleSpatialIndex O(n) with RBush O(log n)
- Bulk loading: ~1.6ms for 5k items
- 10x faster viewport queries expected
- Commit: `cd6da27`

**Files Created:**
- `entity-store/types.ts` - Type definitions and constants
- `entity-store/EntityStore.ts` - TypedArray storage implementation
- `entity-store/index.ts` - Module exports
- `spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts` - R-tree implementation

## Session: 2025-12-17 (BUG-005 + BUG-009 Fixes) - COMPLETE ✅

### Bug Fixes Completed

**BUG-005: Date Labels Not Visible in TimelineHeader - FIXED**

Problem: Date labels in TimelineHeader were not visible at certain zoom levels (especially MACRO mode).

Root Cause:
1. Gradient overlays (30px width) covered labels due to missing z-index
2. PerformancePanel positioned at `top: 50` overlapped TimelineHeader

Fix:
- Added `zIndex: 2` to labels and background color for readability
- Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px with `zIndex: 1`
- Moved PerformancePanel from "top-left" to "bottom-left" position

**BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance Issue - FIXED**

Problem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.

Root Cause:
- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events
- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation
- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations

Fix:
- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY
- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events
- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`
- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)

Also refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:
- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)
- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` - BUG-005 fix
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` - PerformancePanel position change
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - BUG-009 fix
- `features.json` - Marked BUG-005 and BUG-009 as passes: true

**Commits:**
- `4796878` fix(ui): BUG-005 - date labels now visible in TimelineHeader at all zoom levels
- `910a44b` fix(perf): BUG-009 - trackpad zoom now applies instantly without lag

**Status:** Both bugs fixed and verified via Playwright MCP visual testing.

## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED

**Objective:**
Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.

**Research Conducted:**
- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)

**Key Findings:**

1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling

2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   - Perfect for viewport culling queries

3. **Culling Strategy:**
   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   - No new allocations during pan/zoom

4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data

**Documents Created:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings

**Task Created: PERF-002**

Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling

**Dependency Update:**
- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon

**Performance Targets:**
- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)

**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
- Root cause candidates:
  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
- Solution options:
  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
  - Option B: Update target during animation
  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)

**Files Changed:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
- `features.json`:
  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)

**Alignment Fix (Added):**
- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)
- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder
- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays
- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary

**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED

**Objective:**
Plan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.

**Research Document:**
- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`
- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering
- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile
- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)

**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**

Added comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Foundation:** Create EntityStore with TypedArrays (Structure of Arrays pattern)
2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** Implement RBush R-tree for viewport queries
3. **Phase 3 - UI Bridge:** Connect Engine State to @mms/toolkit (NOT Zustand as PDF suggests)
4. **Phase 4 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops
5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches

**CRITICAL DECISION: @mms/toolkit over Zustand**

The PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@mms/toolkit** (packages/toolkit):
- Already implemented and integrated
- Uses Immer for immutability
- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates
- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan
- `CLAUDE.md` - Added "High-Performance Architecture" section with guidelines

**Performance Targets:**
- 100,000+ entities renderable
- 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- <100MB memory for 100k entities
- <5ms viewport queries
- Zero perceptible GC pauses

**Next Steps:**
- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions
- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized
